# REQ-009 Implementation Plan — Auto-generated group landing page

**Goal:** Auto-generate a minimal static `<group>/index.html` at the root of
each non-empty group, listing all child projects as links, regenerated by
matctl on every operation that touches group membership or a child's
visible metadata. Deploy it via CI so that a single token URL
(`…/<group>/?token=…`) lands a student on a group dashboard covered by the
group-scoped cookie already issued by the Worker.

**Context:** REQ-007 introduced groups and scope-based Worker authorization;
REQ-008 made groups first-class manifest entities with titles and added
`modify` subcommands. Both landed the seams this requirement needs: an
explicit list of groups with human-readable titles (`type: group` entries),
titles on every course/doc entry, and a single post-`save_manifest` site in
every mutating CLI handler where a regeneration hook plugs in cleanly
(REQ-008 plan §D10). REQ-007/008/009 ship together as `v0.5.0`.

**Scope boundary:** Only the pieces listed in REQ-009 §1–§6: an HTML
generator, a regeneration seam wired into every manifest-mutating matctl
command, the `<group>/` directory lifecycle on disk, the CI deploy step,
and doc updates. No Quarto toolchain integration, no brand/SCSS styling
(inline CSS only), no JavaScript, no per-project `description` field, no
renaming support (deferred with REQ-008 §6). The Worker is unchanged.

**Status tracking:** `[ ]` open · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done

---

## Design decisions

### D1 — Generator lives in a new private module `_landing.py`

Keep `cli.py` free of HTML. A new `material_core/_landing.py` exports a
single public entry point:

```python
def regenerate_group(cwd: Path, group: str, manifest: CommentedMap) -> None
```

It inspects the manifest to decide whether the group is now non-empty
(write `<group>/index.html`, create `<group>/` if missing) or empty (remove
`<group>/index.html` and the `<group>/` directory if it's otherwise
empty). Callers pass the already-mutated, already-saved manifest; the
function does not touch `projects.yml`.

HTML rendering is a pure function of `(group_entry, children)` → `str`,
split out as `_render_html(...)` for unit testability. No templating
engine — a small f-string / `"".join(...)` is clearer than pulling in
Jinja for ~40 lines of markup.

### D2 — The regeneration seam is a single helper, called at one line per handler

REQ-008 plan §D10 left the seam at "after `save_manifest`, before the final
`click.echo`" in every manifest-mutating handler. REQ-009 fills it with a
single helper in `cli.py`:

```python
def _regenerate_affected_groups(manifest, *groups: str | None) -> None:
    """Call _landing.regenerate_group for each unique, non-None group."""
```

Every mutating handler resolves which groups are affected (one for add /
remove / title-modify, two for group-change-modify) and passes them in.
Deduplication and `None` filtering happen inside the helper so call sites
stay simple.

### D3 — Which group(s) each handler must regenerate

| Handler | Groups to regenerate |
|---|---|
| `course_add` / `doc_add` | the new entry's `group` (if any) |
| `course_remove` / `doc_remove` | the removed entry's `group` (if any), captured **before** `remove_project` |
| `course_modify --title` / `doc_modify --title` | the entry's current `group` (if any) |
| `course_modify --group X` / `doc_modify --group X` | the **old** group **and** the **new** group (either may be None) |
| `course_modify --title --group X` (combined) | old group and new group (title-only change on old is a no-op there; new gets a fresh page including the new title — both covered by regenerating both) |
| `group_modify --title` | the group itself |
| `group_add` | the group itself — always a no-op on disk (no children yet) |
| `group_remove` | the group itself — always a no-op on disk (must be empty to pass the precondition) |

`group_add` / `group_remove` still pass through `regenerate_group` so the
invariant "every mutation ends with a regenerate call for each affected
group" is mechanical and uniform. The empty-group path writes nothing and
removes nothing (D4).

### D4 — Directory lifecycle derives from "does the group have children?"

`regenerate_group` is fully declarative — its behaviour is a pure function
of the current manifest state for that group:

1. Look up the group entry: if absent or `type != "group"`, raise
   `ClickException` (defensive; callers already validate).
2. Compute `children = dependents_of_group(manifest, group)` (already
   exists in `_projects.py`, returns `course`/`doc` names in manifest
   order).
3. **If `children` is non-empty**: ensure `<group>/` exists (`mkdir
   parents=False, exist_ok=True`), render HTML, write
   `<group>/index.html` via an atomic `.tmp` + `rename` pattern.
4. **If `children` is empty**: if `<group>/index.html` exists, delete it;
   if `<group>/` then exists and is empty, `rmdir` it. If `<group>/`
   contains anything else (user stash, stray file), leave it alone and
   print a one-line warning — this matches REQ-008's "loud on unexpected
   state" posture.

This keeps the on-disk invariant (REQ-009 §2): `<group>/` exists ⇔ the
group has at least one child.

### D5 — HTML is hand-written, inline-styled, ASCII-safe

No external assets, no `shared/` link, no JavaScript. Structure matches
REQ-009 §3:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>{group_title}</title>
  <style>/* ~15 lines of minimal CSS */</style>
</head>
<body>
  <main>
    <h1>{group_title}</h1>
    <section>
      <h2>Courses</h2>
      <ul>
        <li><a href="./{name}/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">{title}</a></li>
        ...
      </ul>
    </section>
    <section>
      <h2>Documents</h2>
      ...
    </section>
  </main>
</body>
</html>
```

- Only sections that are non-empty are emitted (REQ-009 §3). If the group
  has only courses, no `<section><h2>Documents</h2>…` is rendered.
- All user-supplied strings (`group_title`, per-project `title`, `name`)
  are escaped via `html.escape(..., quote=True)` before interpolation. No
  f-string-with-unescaped-values anywhere.
- Names are additionally constrained by `_NAME_RE` so `./{name}/` is
  URL-safe without further encoding, but escape them anyway — defence in
  depth.
- `lang="en"` is a placeholder. A follow-up could read a manifest-level
  `lang` field; not in scope here. German titles render fine regardless.
- Inline CSS: system font stack, `max-width: 40rem`, readable line
  height, `margin: 2rem auto`, modest heading sizes. Deliberately
  austere.

### D6 — Ordering: manifest order within type, courses before docs

Per REQ-009 §3 and the acceptance criteria. Implementation: iterate
`manifest["projects"]` once, split into `courses = []` and `docs = []`
lists preserving append order, filter to entries matching
`entry.get("group") == group`. Two passes over one list; O(n), n small.

### D7 — Atomic write, best-effort cleanup

Write via `tmp = target.with_suffix(".html.tmp"); tmp.write_text(html);
tmp.replace(target)` so a crashed matctl never leaves a truncated
`index.html`. On delete, `target.unlink(missing_ok=True)` then
`target.parent.rmdir()` wrapped in a `try/except OSError` that tolerates
`ENOTEMPTY` — a non-empty `<group>/` with stray user content is a state
worth surfacing (one-line `click.echo` warning), not one to auto-resolve.

### D8 — No tests committed; manual acceptance is the contract

Consistent with REQ-004..008. `_landing._render_html` is pure and
testable, but adding a test harness to this repo for one function is
disproportionate. The manual acceptance run below (mirrors REQ-009 §Acceptance
criteria) is the verification of record. If a later requirement adds a
test suite, this function is trivially retrofit-testable.

### D9 — CI deploy: a separate job, fanned out over groups

The existing `build` matrix job fans out over *projects* from
`projects.yml` and deploys each to `httpdocs/<deploy-path>/`. Landing
pages don't fit that shape — a group is not a project in the `build`
sense (no render step, different source file). Add a new job `landing`
that:

1. Runs after `build` succeeds (or independently — they don't conflict;
   group deploy targets are non-overlapping with child deploy targets).
2. Enumerates every `type: group` entry whose `<group>/index.html` exists
   in the checkout (committed files only — no generation in CI).
3. `scp`s each to `httpdocs/<group>/index.html` with `mkdir -p`.

Running after `build` means: if a child project has just been created,
the new child directory exists on Netcup before the landing page links to
it. Not strictly required for correctness (the link would 404 briefly
otherwise, but the child page is the second hop and the landing page is
the first the user sees), but matches the intent.

Skip-logic: if no group has a committed `index.html`, the job is a no-op.
Determined in bash by checking each group's `index.html` with `[ -f … ]`
before scp.

### D10 — `matctl link` / `unlink` unaffected (REQ-009 §5)

The generated `index.html` uses no `_brand.yml` or `shared/` references,
so `link` / `unlink` don't see it. `.gitignore` in `material/` has no
rule that would catch `index.html` at the group level — the file is a
committed artefact, checked in deliberately. Document that explicitly
(§Phase 5).

### D11 — No forward-compatibility shim beyond REQ-009

No plumbing for a future `description` field, branded styling, or
alternative ordering rules. When any of those land, this module gets
extended; the seam in `cli.py` doesn't change.

---

## Phase 1 — `_landing.py` HTML generator

All edits in new file `material_core/_landing.py`.

- [ ] **1.1** Add module docstring, imports (`html`, `pathlib.Path`,
      `click` for the `ClickException` on unexpected states,
      `CommentedMap` type, `dependents_of_group` and `find_entry` from
      `._projects`).
- [ ] **1.2** `_render_html(group_title: str, courses: list[tuple[str,
      str]], docs: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str` — pure function, no
      I/O. Each list is `[(name, title), ...]` in manifest order.
      `html.escape(..., quote=True)` on every interpolated value. Omits
      a section when its list is empty. Inline `<style>` block matching
      §D5.
- [ ] **1.3** `regenerate_group(cwd: Path, group: str, manifest:
      CommentedMap) -> None`:
      - Locate group entry with `find_entry`; error if missing or
        non-group.
      - `children_names = dependents_of_group(manifest, group)`.
      - Partition children into `courses` / `docs` by looking up each
        entry's type (reuse `find_entry`); collect `(name, title)` pairs
        in manifest order.
      - Compute `group_dir = cwd / group`, `target = group_dir /
        "index.html"`.
      - **Non-empty branch**: `group_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)` (no
        `parents`; `cwd` must exist). Atomic write via `.tmp` +
        `rename`.
      - **Empty branch**: `target.unlink(missing_ok=True)`; if
        `group_dir.exists()`, `try: group_dir.rmdir() except OSError:
        click.echo("warning: <group>/ not removed — non-empty")`.
- [ ] **1.4** Export `regenerate_group` in the module's implicit public
      surface (no `__all__` elsewhere in the package; follow the local
      style).

## Phase 2 — CLI wiring

All edits in `material_core/cli.py`.

- [ ] **2.1** Import: `from ._landing import regenerate_group`.
- [ ] **2.2** Add helper at module scope:
      ```python
      def _regenerate_affected_groups(
          manifest: CommentedMap, *groups: str | None
      ) -> None:
          seen: set[str] = set()
          for g in groups:
              if g and g not in seen:
                  seen.add(g)
                  regenerate_group(Path.cwd(), g, manifest)
      ```
- [ ] **2.3** `_scaffold_project` — after `save_manifest`, before the
      final `click.echo`, call `_regenerate_affected_groups(manifest,
      group)`.
- [ ] **2.4** `_remove_project` — `group` is already captured before
      `remove_project`. After `save_manifest`, call
      `_regenerate_affected_groups(manifest, group)`.
- [ ] **2.5** `_modify_project` — record `old_group = entry.get("group")`
      **before** any mutation. After `save_manifest`:
      - If `group is _UNSET` (title-only change): regenerate
        `old_group`.
      - Else (group changed, possibly to/from None): regenerate both
        `old_group` and the new value (`entry.get("group")` after the
        mutation, or `None` if it was removed).
- [ ] **2.6** `group_add` — after `save_manifest`, call
      `regenerate_group(Path.cwd(), name, manifest)` directly. No
      children yet → no-op on disk, but maintains the invariant.
- [ ] **2.7** `group_remove` — after `save_manifest`, call
      `regenerate_group(Path.cwd(), name, manifest)`. The group entry is
      gone, so `find_entry` returns None and the function would error.
      Special-case: for `group_remove`, skip the regeneration call (the
      group has been deleted; there is nothing to regenerate and the
      on-disk path was already empty by the `dependents_of_group`
      precondition). Documented as an exception to the uniform rule.
- [ ] **2.8** `group_modify` — after `save_manifest`, call
      `regenerate_group(Path.cwd(), name, manifest)` to update the
      `<h1>` / `<title>` on disk.

## Phase 3 — `.gitignore` and related housekeeping in `material`

- [ ] **3.1** Inspect `material/.gitignore` (if present) to ensure no
      rule would exclude `*/index.html` at group scope. None expected;
      leave as-is if clean.
- [ ] **3.2** Add a README note (or comment in `projects.yml`) is
      **not** part of this requirement — doc updates in Phase 5 are
      sufficient.

## Phase 4 — CI deploy in `material/.github/workflows/publish.yml`

- [ ] **4.1** Add a new job `landing` after `build`:
      ```yaml
      landing:
        needs: build
        if: always() && needs.build.result != 'failure'
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@v4
          - name: Install yq
            run: sudo snap install yq  # or use the pre-installed one
          - name: Deploy group landing pages
            env:
              SSHPASS: ${{ secrets.SSH_PASSWORD }}
            run: |
              sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq sshpass
              SSH_OPTS="-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -4"
              REMOTE="${{ secrets.SSH_USER }}@${{ secrets.SSH_HOST }}"
              WEBROOT_BASE="/material.professorfroehlich.de/httpdocs"
              mapfile -t groups < <(
                yq -r '.projects[] | select(.type == "group") | .name' projects.yml
              )
              for g in "${groups[@]}"; do
                src="${g}/index.html"
                [ -f "$src" ] || { echo "skip $g (no $src)"; continue; }
                sshpass -e ssh $SSH_OPTS "$REMOTE" "mkdir -p ${WEBROOT_BASE}/${g}"
                sshpass -e scp $SSH_OPTS "$src" "$REMOTE:${WEBROOT_BASE}/${g}/index.html"
              done
      ```
- [ ] **4.2** `if: always() && needs.build.result != 'failure'` so that
      a no-op `build` (empty `projects` matrix — no child changed)
      still deploys refreshed landing pages if `index.html` itself was
      the only thing committed. Note: the current `changes` job forces
      a full rebuild when `projects.yml` changes, so a grouping edit
      also re-renders the children. A pure landing-page edit (e.g.
      cosmetic CSS change after a matctl version bump that re-generated
      every page) would otherwise skip; this `if` covers it.
- [ ] **4.3** Order vs. `build`: `needs: build` so the child deploy
      paths exist before the landing page links to them (D9). The
      `always()` guard ensures landing still deploys when `build` is a
      no-op.

## Phase 5 — Documentation

- [ ] **5.1** `docs/administration.md` — add a new subsection under the
      Groups / Lifecycle area documenting: what the landing page is,
      the on-disk invariant (`<group>/` exists ⇔ non-empty group), the
      regeneration triggers (table from REQ-009 §1), the URL
      (`https://material.professorfroehlich.de/<group>/`), and the CI
      deploy step. One paragraph + the table.
- [ ] **5.2** `docs/authoring.md` — add a brief note where the
      `--group` flag is introduced: "grouped materials share a landing
      page at `<group>/`; the token URL you distribute points there,
      not at an individual child."
- [ ] **5.3** `CLAUDE.md` — in the repository-layout section note the
      generated `material/<group>/index.html` as a committed artefact
      produced by matctl. In the matctl CLI section, add a one-line
      note under each affected command that it regenerates
      `<group>/index.html`.
- [ ] **5.4** `CLAUDE.md` Current status — add REQ-009 DONE line at
      release time.

## Phase 6 — `material` repo: first-time landing-page generation

- [ ] **6.1** After matctl changes land locally (editable pipx install),
      run in `/home/peter/material/`:
      ```bash
      matctl group modify mk4-26 --title "Mikrocomputertechnik 4 — SS 2026"
      ```
      (no-op on the title, but triggers regeneration) — or equivalent
      incantation that exercises the seam. Simpler: add a one-shot
      `matctl group regenerate <name>` command? **Rejected** — not in
      REQ-009, and any `modify` / `add` / `remove` in a follow-up session
      will regenerate. For the initial rollout, run the `group modify`
      no-op or manually delete and re-add one child. Document the chosen
      approach in the release notes.
- [ ] **6.2** Verify `mk4-26/index.html` was created, review in a
      browser locally, commit as part of the batched release commit
      described in REQ-009 §7.

## Phase 7 — Release and close-out (batched `v0.5.0`)

This is the single release step for REQ-007 + REQ-008 + REQ-009. Follows
the checklist already captured in `REQ-009.md §7` and the
`project_req007_release_deferred.md` memory entry.

- [ ] **7.1** `material-core`: ensure `pyproject.toml` version is
      `0.5.0`. Commit REQ-009 Phases 1–5.
- [ ] **7.2** Tag `v0.5.0` on `material-core`, push the tag.
- [ ] **7.3** `material`: commit `projects.yml` + `publish.yml` +
      `mk4-26/index.html` in one commit. Push.
- [ ] **7.4** Cloudflare Worker: redeploy via the dashboard (REQ-007
      change, already finalized — no further Worker edits in REQ-009).
- [ ] **7.5** Run the combined manual acceptance criteria: REQ-007
      A1–A11, REQ-008 B1–B15, REQ-009 C1–C11 below.
- [ ] **7.6** Tick acceptance criteria in `docs/requirements/REQ-009.md`,
      set `Status: DONE`, fill `Completed` and `Verified by`.
- [ ] **7.7** Update `REQUIREMENTS_INDEX.md` to match.
- [ ] **7.8** Remove the `project_req007_release_deferred.md` memory
      entry once the release is cut.

## Manual acceptance run (maps to REQ-009 §Acceptance criteria)

Run in a throwaway checkout with a minimal `projects.yml` that starts
empty (`projects: []`).

- [ ] **C1** `matctl group add mk4-26 --title "Mikrocomputertechnik 4"`
      — no `mk4-26/` directory exists in the tree.
- [ ] **C2** `matctl course add digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik --group
      mk4-26 --title "Digital- und Mikrocomputertechnik"` —
      `mk4-26/index.html` exists, has `<h1>Mikrocomputertechnik 4</h1>`,
      contains a "Courses" section with one `<li><a
      href="./digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik/" target="_blank"
      rel="noopener noreferrer">Digital- und
      Mikrocomputertechnik</a></li>`, no "Documents" section.
- [ ] **C3** `matctl doc add esp-survival-guide --group mk4-26 --title
      "ESP32 Survival Guide"` — `mk4-26/index.html` now lists the
      course under "Courses" and the doc under "Documents", **in that
      order** (Courses section precedes Documents).
- [ ] **C4** `matctl course modify digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik
      --title "New Course Title"` — the link text in
      `mk4-26/index.html` now reads "New Course Title"; the link href
      is unchanged.
- [ ] **C5** `matctl group modify mk4-26 --title "New Group Title"` —
      `<title>` and `<h1>` of `mk4-26/index.html` both update; child
      entries unchanged.
- [ ] **C6** `matctl group add mk4-27 --title "MK4 27"` followed by
      `matctl course modify digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik --group
      mk4-27` — `mk4-26/index.html` no longer lists the course (now
      has only the doc); `mk4-27/index.html` exists and lists the
      course.
- [ ] **C7** `matctl doc modify esp-survival-guide --group ""` —
      `mk4-26/` directory and its `index.html` are deleted (no children
      left); `mk4-27/` is unaffected.
- [ ] **C8** `matctl course modify digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik
      --group ""` — `mk4-27/` directory and its `index.html` are
      deleted.
- [ ] **C9** Inspect any generated `index.html`: every `<a>` has
      `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`; every `href`
      is `./<name>/` (relative, trailing slash).
- [ ] **C10** Add three courses in a specific manifest order, then edit
      `projects.yml` by hand to reorder them, then run a title-only
      `matctl course modify` on one — the regenerated HTML reflects
      the new manifest order.
- [ ] **C11** After a clean `git pull` with a committed `mk4-26/
      index.html` in `material`, push to trigger CI: the `landing` job
      succeeds and `https://material.professorfroehlich.de/mk4-26/`
      serves the landing page. With a valid `mk4-26` token, the page
      loads; each link click opens the child in a new tab without a
      second token prompt.

---

## Commit strategy

`material-core`:

1. **Phase 1** — `_landing.py` generator. One focused commit; no
   behaviour change yet (not wired into CLI).
2. **Phase 2** — CLI wiring. One commit. This is where REQ-009's
   user-visible behaviour lands.
3. **Phase 5** — documentation. Separate so doc-only review stays light.
4. **Phase 7** — REQ status + index + tag.

`material` (batched release commit per REQ-009 §7):

- Single commit containing: `publish.yml` landing job (Phase 4),
  `mk4-26/index.html` (from Phase 6), any documentation touch-ups.

## Risks and mitigations

- **HTML-escaping miss.** A group or project title containing `<`, `>`,
  `&`, `"`, or `'` could inject markup. Mitigation: `html.escape(...,
  quote=True)` on every interpolation (D5). The alternative of trusting
  `_NAME_RE` is insufficient — names are URL-safe but titles are free
  text.
- **Stray files in `<group>/`.** If a user has put a file into the
  group directory, the empty-branch cleanup will not remove the
  directory. Mitigation: soft warning, not an error (D4). `rmdir`
  failing with `ENOTEMPTY` is the signal.
- **Atomic write on Windows.** `Path.replace` is atomic on POSIX and
  documented-best-effort on Windows. matctl is Linux/macOS-only in
  practice; no special casing.
- **CI `landing` job runs before a new child's `build` deploy
  completes.** `needs: build` orders them. `if: always() &&
  needs.build.result != 'failure'` still runs landing when `build` is a
  no-op matrix, which is what we want for pure-landing pushes.
- **First-run regeneration for the existing `mk4-26/` group.** On the
  initial `v0.5.0` rollout, no existing handler will have fired the
  regeneration for `mk4-26`. Phase 6 calls out the one-shot — either a
  no-op `group modify --title` or a deliberate add/remove cycle —
  needed to bootstrap the first `index.html`.
- **Deleted group dir with untracked user content.** Warning path (D4)
  surfaces this, but a user could still lose the file if they later
  `git clean -fd`. Out of scope — matctl cannot protect against user
  deletions it did not make.
- **Click handlers that raise between mutation and regeneration.** If
  `_rewrite_title` raises after the entry has been mutated in memory
  but before `save_manifest`, no on-disk state has changed. If
  `save_manifest` succeeds but `regenerate_group` raises, the manifest
  and the landing page can drift by one edit. Mitigation: the next
  mutating command regenerates — the state is eventually consistent.
  A user wanting to force resync can run any `group modify --title`
  no-op.

## Explicitly out of scope (deferred or covered elsewhere)

- Branded styling (pulling in `_brand.yml` / SCSS) — a future
  requirement if needed. Landing pages are intentionally austere for
  now (REQ-009 §3).
- `subtitle` or a richer `description` field on the landing page —
  REQ-009 Notes.
- A standalone `matctl group regenerate <name>` command — not needed
  given the any-modify-triggers-regen design. Reconsider if a manual
  resync becomes a frequent need.
- Worker changes — none required; the group-scoped cookie from REQ-007
  already covers `<group>/index.html`.
- Rename support — deferred with REQ-008 §6.
- Automated tests — consistent with the rest of matctl; manual
  acceptance is the contract (D8).
